documentation.value: Formats using the default formats for its operands and writes to standard output. Spaces are always added between operands and a newline is appended.
Values to print, separated by spaces.
Formats according to a format specifier and returns the resulting string.
A format string with verbs like %s, %d, %v, etc.
Values to format into the string.

signatures.label: fmt.Fprintf(w io.Writer, format string, a ...any): (n int, err error)

documentation.value: Formats according to a format specifier and writes to w.
The writer to write the formatted output to.
A format string with verbs like %s, %d, %v, etc.
Values to format into the string.
Returns the length of v depending on its type: string (number of bytes), array/slice (number of elements), map (number of keys), channel (number of queued elements).
A string, array, slice, map, or channel.
Returns the capacity of v depending on its type: array (number of elements), slice (maximum length the slice can reach), channel (channel buffer capacity).
An array, slice, or channel.

Allocates and initializes a slice, map, or channel. For slices, size specifies the length and optional capacity. For maps, size is an optional initial capacity hint. For channels, size specifies the buffer capacity.
The type of slice, map, or channel to create.
Length (and optional capacity for slices), or buffer size for channels.
Appends elements to the end of a slice. If the slice has sufficient capacity, the destination is resliced; otherwise, a new underlying array is allocated.
The slice to append to.
Elements to append.
Copies elements from a source slice into a destination slice. Returns the number of elements copied, which is the minimum of len(dst) and len(src).
The destination slice.
The source slice.
Deletes the element with the specified key from the map. If the map is nil or there is no such element, delete is a no-op.
The map to delete from.
The key to delete.
Closes a channel, indicating that no more values will be sent on it. Sending to a closed channel causes a panic.
The channel to close.
Stops normal execution of the current goroutine. When a function calls panic, normal execution stops and any deferred functions begin running. The panic value is reported when the program terminates.
The value to pass to the panic.
Allows a program to manage behavior of a panicking goroutine. Must be called inside a deferred function. Returns the panic value if called during panicking, nil otherwise.
Returns a new error that formats as the given text. Each call to New returns a distinct error value even if the text is identical.
The error message text.
Reports whether substr is within s.
The string to search in.
The substring to search for.
Converts a string to an int. Equivalent to ParseInt(s, 10, 0), converted to type int.
The string representation of an integer.
